questions.question: What is the 'Would I Act on This?' test?

options.text: A test to see if a field triggers a decision or automation
A test to see if a field looks professional
A test to see if a field is required by the CRM
A test to see if a field can be auto-filled

questions.explanation: Every custom field must pass this test: if the answer doesn't trigger a decision or automation, delete it. Solo founders don't have time for vanity metrics.

questions.question: Why are free-text notes problematic for AI agents?

options.text: They take too long to write
They're invisible to AI — agents can't extract structured insights

questions.explanation: Free-text notes like 'Had a good call' are subjective and unstructured. AI agents need categorical fields (event_type, event_outcome, next_action) to reason on.

questions.question: What's the benefit of logging events in a timeline (vs. just updating deal stage)?

options.text: It creates a reasoning chain for AI agents to predict outcomes

questions.explanation: A timeline of events (email_sent → email_received → call → meeting) tells a story. AI agents read stories to predict outcomes and suggest next actions.

questions.question: Why use snake_case field names (e.g., 'fit_score') instead of 'Fit Score'?

options.text: It's required for API compatibility and Course 27 agent integration

questions.explanation: snake_case with category prefixes (fit_score, deal_priority, health_days_since_contact) makes fields machine-readable for AI agents and API integrations.

questions.question: Which of these is a 'health indicator' field?
questions.explanation: Health indicators are auto-calculated signals like 'Days Since Last Contact,' 'Engagement Trend,' and 'Avg Response Time.' They give AI agents real-time deal health data.
